stops.name: The Dubai Mall
stops.shortDescription: One of the world's largest shopping malls, featuring over 1,200 retail outlets. It also houses an Olympic-sized ice rink and a massive indoor aquarium.

stops.name: The Dubai Fountain
stops.shortDescription: The world's largest choreographed fountain system, set on the 30-acre artificial Burj Khalifa Lake. It shoots water up to 500 feet in the air accompanied by lights and music.

stops.name: Souk Al Bahar
stops.shortDescription: An Arabesque dining and lifestyle destination blending classic Middle Eastern architecture with modern luxury. The 'Sailor's Market' offers spectacular waterfront views.

stops.name: Burj Khalifa
stops.shortDescription: Standing at a staggering 828 meters, this is the tallest building in the world. Its design is inspired by the regional Hymenocallis desert flower.

stops.name: Dubai Opera
stops.shortDescription: A radiant center of culture and arts shaped like a traditional dhow boat. This multi-format venue can transform from a theater into a concert hall or a flat-floor event space.

stops.name: Burj Park
stops.shortDescription: A lush green island set against the imposing skyline of Downtown Dubai. It offers an unobstructed, peaceful vantage point of the towering Burj Khalifa above.
